author: @dylanl2258
text: I've been thinking for a while that trees  could be used for generation by connecting them with a sturdy cable, and have a super high torque generator strung between them. When the trees sway in the wind, you'd be able collect some chunk of energy from the entirety of the sway created by their heights and wind drag. If you used a rigid pole (with enough potential movement along the pole's length to compensate for maximum trunk displacement at whatever height you put it) you could generate when the trees are both moving away from each other, and towards. It'd be pretty cheap to make, and could be made at home.

author: @rpned9440
text: Hi, ever thought of modifying a Car Alternator by replacing the armature coil with two magnets out of a microwave oscillator? Did this some years ago, hooked it up to Honda motor, it produced 13.2V. Unfortunately the rig was lost in fire so had other fish to fry.

author: @theodorebelmont2181
text:
Hi Robert, you might be interested in Steve Moulds most recent video (about the Wirtz pump) where he shows Jonathan Deane’s stream powered pump.
Essentially, it’s a waterwheel that pumps water up (8m in that case) which means that you could generate power in the moment and store some for later (with a gravity battery)

author: @Mr.Unacceptable
text: You could make one of these but instead of a button use the wind. While camping. A branch will wave back and forth in the wind. Or a stream of water could be rigged to push the spring and return. Then you have a charger you can use almost anywhere. Combine 3 of them to get 12V and then you have something.

author: @willcool713
text: There was a top my aunt from Germany sent me when I was little, that worked similarly, except the handle pushed down to make the top spin. Just a few fast pumps could spin the top for several minutes. But it had a twisted bar that the handle was attached to, a worm drive of some sort that you pumped to make it spin, and the top was heavy enough to act like a flywheel to maintain the stability and stay upright. I've tried to imagine the internal set up many times, because for hand power generation this seems a way better solution than those hand crank, energency radio chargers. You could pump it fast or slow, and it still sped the top up, no matter how fast it was already going, you just had to be steady. You could keep it spinning as long as you wanted, mostly. There was a shallow wooden bowl that my mom kept bananas in which would keep the top centered. It could've spun in that bowl for hours -- forever really, if I'd wanted to keep it going.

replies.author: @daveamies5031
replies.text: Video's 2005, 2006, 2046 and 2047 will interest you (all about that type of top and it's mechanism) he even provides files if you want to 3D print one.

replies.author: @willcool713
replies.text: @daveamies5031 Thanks! That makes it super easy.

replies.author: @willcool713
replies.text: ​@daveamies5031Yeah, thanks. I went and watched those vids -- somehow I missed that entire set. Interesting that my experiential memory fits the video, but my perceptual memory led me to believe it had a much different mechanism than a simple ratchet. No wonder I couldn't figure it out. I don't remember mine making anything but a bit of a scraping noise, though, aside from some chimes -- must've been some kind of friction ratchet. Thanks again.
